cards.title: Authoritative Build Check
cards.why: Stop GPT from editing the wrong source of truth.
hard_reminders: Identify the exact latest passing runtime-tested build before editing.
Do not use an old draft, canvas-only draft, or guessed file as authoritative.
Do not overwrite the last known good build.

cards.title: Request Scope Lock
cards.why: Prevent collateral edits and make preserved systems explicit.
hard_reminders: Edit only approved files unless a required dependency forces expansion.
List controls, UI rules, launch routing, and asset paths that must survive unchanged.
Name neighboring regression risks before touching code.

cards.title: Candidate Workspace Only
cards.why: Keep the authoritative build insulated from unverified edits.
hard_reminders: Never patch the authoritative tree in place.
Edit only the candidate clone.
Promotion happens only after all gates pass.

cards.title: Pre-Edit Backup
cards.why: Preserve rollback safety before risky changes land.
hard_reminders: Create or verify a rollback snapshot before editing.
Do not treat an unverified copy as a validated backup.
Record the backup location or snapshot identifier.

cards.title: Edit Execution
cards.why: Constrain GPT to the requested change and its justified dependencies.
hard_reminders: Make the smallest viable change first.
Avoid unrelated rewrites, renames, moves, or structure changes unless requested.
Record every touched file honestly.

cards.title: Static Validation
cards.why: Catch syntax, imports, asset paths, and layout risks before runtime.
hard_reminders: Run static checks before runtime claims.
Do not ignore validator failures because the edit seems small.
Record unknown and skipped states explicitly.

cards.title: Runtime Validation Required
cards.why: Stop GPT from claiming completion based only on reading code.
hard_reminders: Do not claim success from code inspection alone.
Do not promote candidate code without a runtime test when runnable.
Do not ignore fresh crash logs.
Use the latest passing tested build as authority.

cards.title: Screenshot Proof
cards.why: Visual work needs current evidence from the latest tested run.
hard_reminders: Capture screenshot proof from the latest validated run when visuals matter.
Do not reuse outdated screenshots for a new build.
If no screenshot is possible, document the reason explicitly.

cards.title: Promotion Gate
cards.why: Only fully validated candidates may replace the authoritative build.
hard_reminders: Do not replace the authoritative build until all prior steps pass.
Preserve failed candidates separately for debugging.
Promotion must be explicit, never implied.

cards.title: Delivery Gate
cards.why: Stop unfinished or weakly validated work from being presented as done.
hard_reminders: Do not present unfinished work as finished.
Do not ship if the latest test produced a crash log.
Clearly distinguish changed, preserved, tested, and unresolved items.
Provide screenshot proof when visuals matter and testing was possible.
